title: Define incoming/ triage: separate signals from work lugs in AI response
problem: The incoming/ folder receives both signals (type: signal — knowledge broadcasts for adoption) and work lugs (type: impl, task, spec — actionable items). When an AI is asked to 'check incoming lugs', it presents all items as equivalent work, burying the one real lug under a wall of signals. This happens on every spoke because the disambiguation rule is not specified in the wakeup or triage protocol.
evidence: basher session-65: incoming/ had 1 impl lug and 12 signals. AI presented all 13 as 'incoming lugs to implement', requiring the user to correct the framing.
value: Eliminates a recurring UX failure. User asks about lugs, gets a signal briefing. Signals and lugs require different responses — signals need adoption decisions; lugs need planning and implementation.
acceptance_criteria: WAI triage protocol (wai.md or a referenced triage spec) defines the two categories in incoming/: (1) work lugs (type: impl, task, spec, bug, feature) and (2) signals (type: signal)
When presenting incoming/ contents, the AI leads with work lugs count and titles, then lists signals separately as 'X signals pending adoption'
The wakeup brief or triage step explicitly states: 'signals are not lugs — do not present them as actionable work items'
Spoke template or triage command updated to reflect this distinction
suggested_fix: Add a triage rule to the incoming/ processing step: filter by type field. type in [impl, task, spec, bug, feature] = work lug (plan and implement). type == signal = signal (adopt or defer). Present them in separate sections with separate counts.
resolution: Triage Presentation Rule added to templates/commands/wai-full.md Incoming Routing Rules section. Separates work lugs from signals in presentation format.
